articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5 in late March 2026, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in long-horizon planning tasks and mathematical proof generation, narrowing the gap with domain-specific systems. Enterprise API availability began rolling out in early April with tiered pricing based on context utilization.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Ultra 2 Achieves State-of-the-Art on Scientific Benchmarks
articles.summary: DeepMind announced Gemini Ultra 2, which sets new records on GPQA-Diamond, MathOlympiad, and several graduate-level science benchmarks. The model introduces a novel mixture-of-experts architecture that dynamically allocates compute to sub-problems, enabling PhD-level performance on multi-step chemistry and physics reasoning tasks. Google is integrating the model across its Cloud AI Platform and research tools.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Deploys Humanoid Workers at BMW Spartanburg Plant at Scale
articles.summary: Figure AI announced that over 200 of its Figure 03 humanoid robots are now operating across multiple assembly lines at BMW's Spartanburg, South Carolina plant, marking the largest commercial deployment of general-purpose humanoid robots in manufacturing. The robots handle bin-picking, parts transfer, and quality inspection tasks with a reported 99.2% uptime. BMW says the deployment has reduced certain line cycle times by 18%.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Demonstrates Autonomous Construction Site Navigation and Tool Use
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics released a technical demo showing its electric Atlas robot autonomously navigating an active construction site, identifying tasks from a digital work order, selecting appropriate power tools, and performing drilling and fastening operations. The system uses a vision-language-action model fine-tuned on construction domain data. The company announced partnerships with Bechtel and Skanska for pilot deployments starting Q3 2026.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces 'Constitutional Classifiers' Framework to Mitigate Jailbreaks at the Infrastructure Level
articles.summary: Anthropic published a paper and open-source toolkit for Constitutional Classifiers, a defense-in-depth approach that embeds safety classifiers directly into the inference pipeline at multiple transformer layers rather than relying solely on output filtering. Internal red-team evaluations showed a 94% reduction in successful jailbreak attacks compared to output-only safeguards, with less than 2% overhead on latency. The framework is model-agnostic and has been adopted by two major cloud providers.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of A14 2nm Node with Backside Power Delivery
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed volume production has started on its N2 process node at its Kaohsiung fab, featuring gate-all-around nanosheet transistors and backside power delivery network technology. Early yields are reportedly exceeding initial projections, with Apple and Nvidia among the first customers. The node delivers approximately 25% speed improvement or 30% power reduction over N3E, critical for next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface with 4x Electrode Density
articles.summary: Neuralink announced FDA clearance for its N2 implant, which features 4,096 electrodes—four times the density of its first-generation device—and a wireless data throughput of 200 Mbps. The improved resolution enables more granular motor cortex decoding, and early trial participants with spinal cord injuries demonstrated cursor control speeds approaching able-bodied benchmarks. The company plans to expand its clinical trial to 50 participants across five US hospitals.

articles.title: Toyota Research Institute Unveils Diffusion-Policy-Based Robotic Manipulation System That Learns from 10 Minutes of Demo Data
articles.summary: TRI presented a new manipulation framework at ICRA 2026 that uses diffusion policy models to learn complex bimanual manipulation tasks—including cable routing, fabric folding, and liquid pouring—from as few as 10 minutes of human teleoperation data. The system generalizes to novel object geometries with an 87% success rate, a major step toward practical few-shot robot learning. TRI plans to open-source the training pipeline and a simulated benchmark suite.
